text.title: ops-annual-planning-templates Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for ops-annual-planning-templates. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Fill templates in this order: vision (human only) → strategic priorities (human writes, agent critiques) → annual goals (must have numeric baseline and target) → quarterly breakdown → budget table → not-doing list. Never start with the budget or task list.
rule.rationale: Strategy must drive budgets and tasks, not the reverse. Filling budget first anchors the plan to available spend; filling tasks first anchors to current activity. Both undermine strategic clarity.

rule.statement: Every field an agent cannot ground in provided input data must be left as "[NEEDS HUMAN INPUT: reason]". Agents must not invent metrics, goals, or financials when input data is missing.
rule.rationale: Plausible-sounding but invented data in a planning template is more harmful than empty fields. A founder who thinks their CAC is $150 because an LLM generated that number will make wrong budget decisions.

rule.statement: The "Not Doing This Year" section must contain at least 3 concrete items. If a human skips this section, require them to fill it before accepting the plan as complete.
rule.rationale: The stop-doing list is the most value-producing section and the most consistently skipped. Without it, the plan adds new priorities on top of existing commitments, which is how founders burn out.

rule.statement: Reject any Key Result that: (a) uses activity language ("launch", "build", "ship") instead of outcome language, (b) has no numeric target, or (c) lacks a baseline and measurement source. Require a rewrite before accepting.
rule.rationale: Activity KRs measure effort, not impact. A team can ship 5 features and still lose customers. Outcome KRs require the team to think about what "done successfully" looks like from the customer's perspective.

rule.statement: Every metric in a filled template must be traceable to a provided input. If the input data does not contain a value, leave the field as "[NEEDS HUMAN INPUT]" with a reason. Do not extrapolate or estimate without labeling estimates as estimates.
rule.rationale: Planning templates filled with LLM-generated plausible numbers produce plans that look complete but are grounded in fiction. Founders make real budget and hiring decisions from these plans.

rule.statement: When filling the quarterly review "misses" section, require structural framings ("we under-resourced X" or "we deprioritized Y for Z") rather than external-attribution framings ("market conditions" or "timing was off"). Reject or flag external attribution without structural cause.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc rationalization using external causes prevents learning. Structural causes are actionable; market condition causes are not.

rule.statement: Run the OKR validator (okr-validator.py) on every set of key results before accepting a plan as final. Do not manually review — run the validator and surface all failures before proceeding.
rule.rationale: Manual review of OKR quality is inconsistent. The validator applies uniform rules and catches the most common failure modes (activity KRs, missing numbers, missing baselines) mechanically.

rule.statement: Year-in-review documents must not be published or shared externally without explicit founder approval. These artifacts contain financial data, failure modes, and personal reflection that are vulnerability disclosures if released publicly.
rule.rationale: Year-in-review posts by founders are often the primary signal competitors use for strategic intelligence. The agent should never auto-publish or share these documents.
